query: What is the most recent NIFC National Fire News year-to-date acres burned figure for 2026, latest available as of late June 2026? Report the exact YTD acres and date.
answer:
The most recent NIFC National Fire News year-to-date acres burned figure for 2026 is **3,197,098 acres** (with 36,463 year-to-date wildfires).

**Exact date:** This figure comes from the **Incident Management Situation Report (IMSR) dated Thursday, July 2, 2026 – 0730 MDT**, which is the most recent available data as of late June / early July 2026. The NIFC National Fire News narrative page carried an "as of June 29, 2026" banner, but its statistics feed had already updated to reflect the July 2, 2026 IMSR value of 3,197,098 acres.

All three NIFC sources (the IMSR PDF, the National Fire News page, and the Statistics page) agree on this figure. For context, it represents 154% of the 2016–2025 ten-year average of 2,072,491 acres, and the two largest regional contributors were the Rocky Mountain Area (1,298,779 acres) and the Southern Area (1,043,020 acres).

key_facts: NIFC year-to-date acres burned for 2026 = 3,197,098 acres (per IMSR dated July 2, 2026 – 0730 MDT).

The figure is drawn from the Incident Management Situation Report (IMSR); the NIFC National Fire News page narrative was stamped 'as of June 29, 2026' but the statistics reflected the July 2, 2026 IMSR.
All three NIFC sources (IMSR PDF, National Fire News page, Statistics page) agree on 3,197,098 acres and 36,463 fires.
2026 YTD acres are 154% of the 2016-2025 ten-year average (2,072,491 acres); YTD fires are 133% of the ten-year average (27,446 fires).
National Preparedness Level was 4; there were 49 uncontained large fires and 9,630 personnel assigned as of July 2, 2026.
Largest regional YTD acreage: Rocky Mountain Area 1,298,779 acres; Southern Area 1,043,020 acres; Great Basin 414,902 acres.
Largest single active fire was the Cottonwood Fire (Fishlake NF, Utah) at 93,918 acres.

data_points.metric: 2026 YTD acres as percent of 2016-2025 ten-year average
